entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The primary crop studied for residue burning and cultivation patterns.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: The global environmental shift driving the need for emission mitigation.

entities.name: GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSIONS
entities.description: The release of gases like CO2, CH4, and N2O into the atmosphere.

entities.name: RESIDUE BURNING
entities.description: The practice of burning agricultural crop remains.

entities.name: RICE CULTIVATION
entities.description: The agricultural process of growing rice.

entities.name: SENTINEL-2 SATELLITE IMAGERY
entities.description: Satellite data used for mapping burn areas and dates.

entities.name: MACHINE LEARNING ALGORITHMS
entities.description: Computational methods used to analyze cultivation patterns and burn areas.

entities.name: GROUND TRUTH DATA
entities.description: Field-verified data used to calibrate and validate satellite analysis.

entities.name: KALESHWARAM INTERVENTION
entities.description: The specific region or project area where rice cultivation changes were analyzed.

entities.name: NEAR-INFRARED REGION (NIR) BAND
entities.description: A specific spectral band used to identify residue burn areas and dates.

entities.name: CO2
entities.description: Carbon dioxide emitted during crop residue burning.

entities.name: CH4
entities.description: Methane emitted during both crop residue burning and rice cultivation.

entities.name: N2O
entities.description: Nitrous oxide emitted during crop residue burning.

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE CROP RESIDUE MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES
entities.description: Approaches designed to reduce the environmental impact of residue burning.
